description_long: This is a four-story brick apartment building with a flat, dark roof and symmetrical window arrangements on its main facades. The front features a small staircase leading to a single main entrance, flanked by a patch of grass and a large tree. The rear side of the building is more ornate, with a series of stacked balconies and arched windows, as well as a landscaped area with potted plants and flowers. The side walls are plain, constructed entirely of brick with no windows or decorative elements. The building's exterior is primarily red brick, with white balcony railings and window frames adding contrast. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and early 20th-century urban residential architecture.
description: A four-story red brick apartment building with symmetrical windows, balconies at the rear, and a landscaped front entrance with a tree.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a staircase, symmetrical windows, and a large tree on the right side.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain brick wall with no windows or decorative features, and part of the landscaped area.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights stacked balconies, arched windows, and a landscaped area with potted plants and flowers.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing a plain brick wall and the continuation of the front steps and tree.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns on the front and rear facades, with consistent spacing and alignment across all floors. The side walls lack windows, emphasizing the building's rectangular, urban form.
